zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Monte Alto於大約1100年出生時，他的父親Ralph de Mohaut32歲，母親Emma de Laval5歲。他於1139年與Leucha FitzNigel結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1177年在英國威爾斯福林爾哈沃登享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Monte Alto于大约1100年出生时，他的父亲Ralph de Mohaut32岁，母亲Emma de Laval5岁。他于1139年与Leucha FitzNigel结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1177年在英国威尔斯福林爾哈沃登享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Monte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Mario, Domenic, Salvatore, Aldo, Angelo, Antonio, Arturo, Carmelo, Gaspare, Carmela, Ciro, Gasper, Philomena.  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Edgardo, Emilia.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese monte (from Latin mons, genitive montis).
Italian and Jewish (from Italy): habitational name from any of the places called with Monte or Monti. Compare   Del Monte  .

variants.name: Monti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Aniello, Pasquale, Aldo, Carmel, Elio, Enrico, Gaetano, Marco, Marino, Philomena.
Italian: variant of   Monte  , from the plural.

variants.name: Montagna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Sal, Gaetano, Alessandro, Antonino, Attilio, Carlo, Carmelo, Cosmo, Dino, Enrico, Oreste.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain or mountains, Italian montagna, from Late Latin montanea, originally the neuter plural of an adjective derivative of mons (see Monte 1), or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word.

variants.name: Montini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Enio, Pietro, Arsenio, Concetta, Dante, Dino, Domenic, Eliseo, Lorenzo, Marco, Mauro, Quirino, Ricco, Tulio.
Italian (Milan):
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Montes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Jorge, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Carlos, Alfredo, Ramon, Raul.  Portuguese Joaquim.

Spanish (also Montés); Portuguese (also Montês): topographic name for a mountain dweller, from the plural form of Monte ‘mountain’ or from the adjective Spanish montés, Portuguese montês ‘of the mountain’.

variants.name: Montello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Biagio, Carmela, Marco.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Del Monte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Angelo, Rocco, Giovanni, Pompeo, Antonio, Armelio, Berta, Camillo, Carmela, Deno, Elvio, Ezio, Gilda, Giulio.  Spanish Angel, Gonzalo, Guillermina, Jesus, Jose, Jose Manuel.
Italian, Spanish, and Jewish (from Italy): variant, with the preposition and definite article del ‘from the’, of Monte .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Montejo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Raul, Carlos, Eduardo, Abelardo, Alberto, Julio, Mario, Mauricio, Miguel, Miguel Angel, Alejandro, Alfonso.
Spanish:
topographic name from a diminutive of   Monte   1.

variants.name: Montesi
meanings: Italian:
habitational name for someone from a place called   Monte  , from an adjectival form of the placename.
from the plural of Montese, a habitational name from Montese in Modena province.

variants.name: Montas
meanings: Spanish and Catalan (Montás); French: derivative of Spanish monte, Catalan and Old French mont ‘mountain’ (see Monte , Mont ). This surname is most common in the Dominican Republic and Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: Alto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Arvo, Reino, Eino, Sulo, Waino.
Altered form of Finnish   Aalto  .
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: nickname for a big man, from alto ‘tall, big’.

variants.name: Aalto
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Tauno, Ensio, Pentti, Reino, Sulo, Tarmo, Wilho.  Scandinavian Hilma, Johan.
Finnish: ornamental name from aalto ‘wave’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Monte Alto was born about 1100, his father, Ralph de Mohaut, was 32 and his mother, Emma de Laval, was 5. He married Leucha FitzNigel in 1139. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died about 1177, in Hawarden, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.Hawarden, Flint, Wales: Hawarden, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Monte Alto est né environ 1100, son père, Ralph de Mohaut, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Emma de Laval, avait 5 ans. Il a épousé Leucha FitzNigel en 1139. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé environ 1177, à Hawarden, Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Monte Alto um 1100 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph de Mohaut 32 und seine Mutter Emma de Laval 5. Er hat 1139 Leucha FitzNigel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb um 1177 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Hawarden, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Monte Alto è nato il circa 1100, il suo padre, Ralph de Mohaut, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Emma de Laval, aveva 5 anni. Lui sposato Leucha FitzNigel nel 1139. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì circa 1177, nel Hawarden, Flintshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Monte Alto(이)가 대략 1100년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph de Mohaut(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma de Laval(은)는 5세였다. 그는 1139년 Leucha FitzNigel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1177년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르 호르든에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
